title: narcissus triandrus var. pulchellus
intro: Narcissus triandrus var. pulchellus, commonly known as Lovers' Knots or Sweet Bulb, is a type of flowering bulbous perennial plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Narcissus triandrus var. pulchellus include its ability to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation, making it a popular remedy for menstrual cramps, arthritis, and other conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant may be made by drying the bulbs and using them in teas, infusions, or as a tincture, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits.
